devnotes.summary: Options for calculation of sink reduction factor for root water uptake
Options for calculation of automatic irrigation
Options for calculation of sink reduction factor based on root length distribution and potential water uptake

Model component for adding root soil water uptake to the simulation of vertical (1D) soil water transport The distribution of water uptake over the soil layers and the calculation of drought limited water uptake can be calculated with different options
internal variable for summing up all sink terms
field for option with or without roots, if false water uptake is calculated without considering root distribution and sink reduction, if true root distribution and sink reduction are considered

field.name: fAutoirriMethod
field.devnotes: field for automatic irrigation method (amTransRatio, amProznFKWe, amProznFKActRootedComps)

devnotes.summary: Option for method of automatic irrigation (amTransRatio, amProznFKWe, amProznFKActRootedComps)
Option for method of sink term calculation (Feddes, Psicrit, Psicrit_corr, nFKcrit, MFP)
Option to write the matrix flux table
Array for soil water content differences between bulk soil and root surface for each soil layer
Matrix potential differences between root surface and bulk soil [cm3/cm3]
Array for proportional nFK values for each soil layer
Array for effective root length density distribution [cm/cm3]

field.name: psi_2
field.devnotes: average, weighted soil water potential within the rooting zone [pF]

field.name: psi_3
field.devnotes: Wasserspannung ab der Wasseraufnahme beginnt abzunehmen

field.name: nfk_threshold
field.devnotes: critical nFK value for triggering irrigation

field.name: Psi2
field.devnotes: Ratio of act. evaporation to pot. evapotranspiration

field.name: act_rooted_comps
field.devnotes: water potential at which water uptake by the plant starts to decrease [hPa]

devnotes.summary: Sink reduction calculation with 5 options 1) Feddes: reduction factor based on soil water tension thresholds and potential transpiration rate following Feddes et al. (1978) 2) Psicrit: reduction factor based on soil water tension threshold (Psi2) following Van Genuchten (1987) 3) nFKcrit: reduction factor based on relative soil water content (nFK) threshold following Van Genuchten (1987) 4) Psicrit_corr: reduction factor based on soil water tension at the root surface, which is calculated based on potential water uptake and root length distribution, and soil water retention curve 5) MFP: reduction factor based on soil water tension at the root surface, which is calculated based on potential water uptake and root length distribution, and soil water retention curve, with a maximum flow principle (MFP) approach for calculating the potential water uptake

procedure.name: CalcRatesAndIntegrate
procedure.devnotes: Calculation of sink reduction factor based on root length density distribution and potential water uptake per layer
